title: Tensorial Forms in Infinite Dimensions
speaker: Andrew Snowden
field_msc: 15 Linear and multilinear algebra; matrix theory
abstract: Let V be a complex vector space and consider symmetric d-linear forms on V, i.e., linear maps Symd(V)→>C. When V is finite dimensional and d>2, the structure of such forms is very complicated. Somewhat surprisingly, when V has countably infinite dimension there is much more order. For instance, there is a unique isomorphism class of form that is ultrahomogeneous (joint work with N. Harman), and (at least for d=3) it is possible to classify forms up to a notion of isogeny (joint work with A. Danelon).
tags: Workshop on Additive Combinatorics and Algebraic Connections

proof_stages.label: High-strength motivation from Stillman
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins by stating Stillman's conjecture, defining strength and collective strength, and explaining the Ananyan--Hochster principle that high collective strength makes bounded-degree polynomials behave like independent variables.

proof_stages.label: Infinite-strength polynomial ring and enough derivations
proof_stages.summary: The finite high-rank principle is pushed to a limiting ring of infinite homogeneous series, where infinite collective strength leads to the theorem that the ring is abstractly polynomial, proved through the criterion of having enough negative-degree derivations.

proof_stages.label: Repackaging tensors as universal \(d\)-spaces
proof_stages.summary: Infinite series are recast as countable-dimensional vector spaces equipped with symmetric \(d\)-linear forms, with embeddings as morphisms, and universality is characterized by infinite strength.

proof_stages.label: Fraisse-style construction of the homogeneous universal tensor
proof_stages.summary: Homogeneity is introduced as extension of finite-dimensional partial isomorphisms, and a linear analogue of Fraisse limits is used to produce a unique countable-dimensional universal homogeneous \(d\)-space.

proof_stages.label: Automorphism groups, linear oligomorphicity, and categoricity
proof_stages.summary: The automorphism group \(G_d\) of the universal homogeneous \(d\)-space is analyzed as a large infinite-dimensional group, with linear analogues of oligomorphicity and omega-categoricity formulated and proved for these spaces.

proof_stages.label: Isogeny classes and residual rank for cubic spaces
proof_stages.summary: Weakening isomorphism to mutual embeddability gives a poset of infinite-strength \(d\)-spaces, and in degree three the residual rank of the span of partial derivatives modulo finite-strength quadratics classifies the isogeny classes as \(\mathbb N\cup\{\infty\}\).
